automotive-ev-battery.title: Is Tesla embellishing statistics for its FSD system?
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Tesla faces scrutiny from European authorities and independent traffic researchers over potentially misleading safety statistics submitted for its Full Self-Driving system in Sweden and the Netherlands. The allegations suggest the automaker may be misrepresenting data to secure regulatory approval for the advanced driver assistance system in Europe.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Tesla Cybercab with 48 kWh battery and front-mounted motor revealed
automotive-ev-battery.summary: EPA documents have revealed detailed specifications for Tesla's Cybercab, disclosing a 163-kW front-mounted motor and a compact 48 kWh battery capacity. The information provides the first comprehensive technical insight into the highly anticipated autonomous vehicle platform unveiled in 2024.

automotive-ev-battery.title: UK Plans To Roll Back EV Mandate
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The United Kingdom is preparing to modify its Zero Emission Vehicle mandate in response to concerns raised by automakers and trade unions regarding implementation feasibility. The regulatory adjustment signals shifting government priorities in response to industry pressure.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Charging platform eMabler secures €5.5 million of funding
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Finnish charging solutions startup eMabler has completed a Series A funding round of over €5.5 million, led by Greencode Ventures with participation from several strategic investors. The platform specializes in B2B charging infrastructure and combined park-and-charge offerings for the European market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Vietnam: VinFast secures an order for 2,000 electric cars
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Vietnamese logistics company Huy Long has signed an agreement to purchase 2,000 electric vehicles from domestic automaker VinFast. The phased deployment order represents a significant commercial milestone for the Vietnamese EV manufacturer in its home market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Xpeng launches X9 electric MPV in seven European markets
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese automaker Xpeng has officially launched its flagship electric multi-purpose vehicle, the X9, across seven European countries including Germany, Finland, and Norway, with customer deliveries already underway. The expansion represents a significant push by the Chinese manufacturer to establish its presence in the European market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: €3,000 for battery defects: Aviloo converts battery diagnostics into a Purchase Guarantee
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Austrian battery analysis specialist Aviloo has expanded its independent battery diagnostic service to include a purchase guarantee covering up to €3,000 compensation for battery defects in used electric vehicles. The offering aims to increase transparency and confidence in the used EV market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Genesis to expand into four additional European countries in 2027
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Hyundai Group's luxury brand Genesis plans to enter four new European markets—Austria, Denmark, Poland, and Portugal—in 2027, following its scheduled Spanish launch in late 2026. The expansion will bring the brand's total European presence to eleven countries.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BYD Taking Responsibility Increases God's Eye Use & Makes Vehicles Safer
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BYD has introduced a comprehensive damage guarantee for autonomous driving features on LiDAR-equipped vehicles, covering both urban driving assistance and advanced parking functions. The initiative reflects the company's commitment to building consumer confidence in autonomous driving technology through financial backing.

automotive-ev-battery.title: New Continental tyres deliver 30km of additional range for Renault
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Continental has developed low rolling resistance tires for Renault electric vehicles that can provide up to 30 kilometers of additional driving range on a 500-kilometer EV. The development highlights the significant impact of tire technology on energy efficiency and overall vehicle performance.

science-space.title: NASA's Webb Catches Exoplanet Getting Roasted
science-space.summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope has captured detailed observations of HD 80606 b, a gas giant four times the mass of Jupiter with an extremely elliptical orbit that brings it dangerously close to its Sun-like star. The research reveals how intense stellar radiation affects this unusual exoplanet's atmosphere and properties. Findings from the study were presented by the research team this week.

science-space.title: James Webb Space Telescope Forecasts Extreme Weather on Exoplanet That Rains Rubies and Sapphires
science-space.summary: The James Webb Space Telescope has provided unprecedented detailed observations of an exoplanet with extreme atmospheric conditions capable of producing crystalline precipitation. The discovery showcases JWST's ability to analyze distant planetary atmospheres in remarkable detail, advancing scientists' understanding of exoplanet weather systems. This research demonstrates the telescope's revolutionary capabilities for studying worlds beyond our solar system.

science-space.title: NASA Webb, Hubble Reveal History of Relic of Milky Way's Formation
science-space.summary: Using both the James Webb and Hubble Space Telescopes, researchers have definitively determined that Terzan 5 is not a globular star cluster as previously classified, revealing new insights into galactic formation and evolution. The joint observation combines the unique capabilities of NASA's two most powerful observatories to reassess this ancient stellar system. This discovery provides important context for understanding how galaxies like the Milky Way developed over billions of years.

science-space.title: NASA X-ray Spacecraft Catches Jet Erupting From 1st Supermassive Black Hole Imaged by Humanity
science-space.summary: NASA's X-ray observatories have detected unprecedented detail in the jets erupting from the first supermassive black hole ever directly imaged by humanity, revealing dynamic changes in the black hole's emission. The enhanced X-ray observations provide scientists with their most detailed view yet of how black hole jets behave and evolve. This research advances understanding of the most extreme objects in the universe.

science-space.title: NASA's Quantum Lab Aboard Space Station Gets Chilly Upgrade
science-space.summary: Astronauts on the International Space Station have activated an upgraded Cold Atom Lab, a specialized facility designed to conduct quantum research impossible to perform on Earth. The lab exploits the unique microgravity environment of space to study fundamental properties of matter and develop advanced quantum technologies. This upgrade represents a significant advancement in orbital science capabilities.

science-space.title: Watch a Fireball Burn a 300 Mile Path Above the Midwest US
science-space.summary: A spectacular fireball meteor traversed a 300-mile path across the Midwest United States sky in mere seconds before breaking apart in a dramatic display. The event was captured on video by multiple observers across the region. This rare celestial event provides valuable data for studying meteoric phenomena and atmospheric interactions.

science-space.title: Aurora Australis
science-space.summary: A striking photograph captured the Aurora Australis illuminating Earth's southern sky during an active solar event on June 5, 2026, as the International Space Station orbited 271 miles above the Indian Ocean near Perth, Australia. The image showcases the colorful display resulting from the interaction of solar particles and Earth's magnetic field. Auroras provide visual evidence of the dynamic space weather environment surrounding our planet.

science-space.title: NASA Uses Machine Learning to Enhance Flash Flood Warnings
science-space.summary: NASA has developed the Transient Artifact and Continuous Learning System (TACLS), which combines satellite data with machine learning algorithms to improve meteorologists' ability to forecast flash floods. The system leverages continuous observations from satellite networks to provide more accurate and timely warnings. This application demonstrates how artificial intelligence enhances operational weather prediction capabilities.

science-space.title: Watch SpaceX Launch 3 Huge BlueBird Direct-to-Cell Satellites From Florida Early on June 17
science-space.summary: SpaceX will launch three BlueBird direct-to-cell satellites for AST SpaceMobile aboard a Falcon 9 rocket from Florida on the morning of June 17, a mission livestream will be available for public viewing. The BlueBird satellites represent advancement in space-based cellular connectivity technology. This launch continues the commercial space industry's expansion into satellite communications infrastructure.

science-space.title: Watch European Rocket Launch Record-Breaking Mission for Amazon on June 17
science-space.summary: An Ariane 6 heavy-lift rocket will launch a record-breaking payload to orbit for Amazon on the morning of June 17, with a live broadcast available for viewers. The mission represents a significant achievement in European launch capabilities and commercial space deployment. This launch demonstrates the international competition in the satellite deployment and space infrastructure markets.

finance-markets.title: SpaceX leapfrogs Amazon to become world's fifth-most valuable company
finance-markets.summary: SpaceX has surpassed Amazon in market valuation to become the world's fifth-most valuable company, bolstered by Elon Musk's artificial intelligence and satellite ventures. The company has also announced a $60 billion acquisition deal for Cursor, an AI coding application.

finance-markets.title: Oil sinks below $80 as traders bet Strait of Hormuz flows will return
finance-markets.summary: Brent crude has plummeted to a three-month low following an agreement to extend the US-Iran ceasefire, with markets anticipating the reopening of critical shipping lanes. The decline reflects expectations that normalised trade flows will increase global oil supply.

finance-markets.title: Bond Rally Fails to Allay Higher-for-Longer Global Rates Threat
finance-markets.summary: Despite recent bond market gains driven by easing geopolitical tensions and lower oil prices, governments worldwide face persistently elevated borrowing costs expected to remain elevated through the remainder of the year. The structural pressures on global interest rates continue to outweigh short-term relief from improved regional stability.

finance-markets.title: Private equity bosses warn of AI threat to bets on law and accountancy
finance-markets.summary: Leading private equity executives have cautioned that artificial intelligence development poses significant disruption risks to their substantial investments in professional services firms. The technology threatens to automate functions traditionally performed by lawyers and accountants, potentially eroding the value of these portfolio holdings.

finance-markets.title: Australia Must Gird for Shock-Prone Financial System, RBA Warns
finance-markets.summary: A senior Reserve Bank of Australia official has warned that domestic financial institutions must prepare for heightened volatility in an increasingly unstable geopolitical environment. The RBA cited strained international relations as reshaping financial and economic linkages that could impact system resilience.

finance-markets.title: China Stock Gauge Sinks as Traders Favor AI Winners Elsewhere
finance-markets.summary: Chinese stocks listed in Hong Kong have reached bleak milestones as global capital flows favour artificial intelligence supply chain players over traditional internet and consumer companies dominating the offshore benchmark. The divergence reflects a structural rotation away from China's traditional growth engines toward AI-adjacent sectors elsewhere.

finance-markets.title: US and Europe discuss access to AI models after Anthropic dispute
finance-markets.summary: The United States and European allies are negotiating a 'trusted partner' scheme that would grant select nations controlled access to cutting-edge artificial intelligence models. The framework emerges following disputes over AI model distribution and reflects broader efforts to balance technological innovation with allied security concerns.

finance-markets.title: Ambani's Jio set to file for IPO as hopes grow for end to India's listings drought
finance-markets.summary: Reliance Industries' Jio division is preparing to file for an initial public offering as sentiment builds around a potential recovery in India's equity capital markets. The geopolitical conflict has dampened India's IPO activity, which had been positioned for another record year.

finance-markets.title: Top Japan executive sounds alarm over 'endlessly' investing in US
finance-markets.summary: A prominent Japanese business executive has raised concerns about Japan's $550 billion funding commitment to American infrastructure projects amid lagging domestic productivity growth. The criticism highlights tensions between capital allocation to foreign markets and domestic economic development needs.

finance-markets.title: Ex-Citi executive says bank sacked her after raising Trump concerns
finance-markets.summary: A former Citigroup executive has filed a lawsuit alleging she was terminated after identifying deficiencies in the bank's risk management practices and raising concerns regarding political considerations. The case highlights tensions between regulatory compliance and institutional decision-making processes.

world-news.title: Iran war live: Israel kills 4 in Lebanon as Trump criticises Netanyahu
world-news.summary: Ongoing military escalation between Israel and Lebanon continues with fresh casualties reported, while incoming US President Trump publicly criticizes Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu's approach to the conflict. The situation underscores deep divisions within the US-Israel alliance and raises questions about future American Middle East policy.

world-news.title: Fragile quiet in Lebanon as US-Iran truce leaves unanswered questions
world-news.summary: A ceasefire agreement between the US and Iran has brought temporary calm to Lebanon, but significant skepticism remains among the Lebanese population regarding its durability and effectiveness. The truce leaves numerous outstanding issues unresolved in the broader regional conflict.

world-news.title: Tehran selling deal with US as victory – but for Iranians it was necessity
world-news.summary: Iran's government frames a recent agreement with the US as a diplomatic success, though ordinary Iranians view it primarily as a pragmatic necessity to avert military confrontation and economic hardship. The disconnect between official messaging and public sentiment reflects deep concerns about war and economic stability.

world-news.title: Smotrich says Israel taking control of Hebron
world-news.summary: Israeli Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich announced that Israel has transferred administrative authority over Hebron to the military, signaling a significant shift in Israeli control over the disputed Palestinian territory. The move represents a major escalation in Israeli territorial assertions in the West Bank.

world-news.title: Venezuela signs deal with US energy giant to rebuild power grid
world-news.summary: Venezuela's interim government has entered into an agreement with General Electric to reconstruct the nation's deteriorating power infrastructure, marking a significant turn toward US corporate engagement. The deal represents a potential thaw in US-Venezuela relations and indicates a shift in the country's economic direction.

world-news.title: Brazilian court convicts Eduardo Bolsonaro of courting US interference
world-news.summary: A Brazilian court has convicted Eduardo Bolsonaro, son of former President Jair Bolsonaro, of attempting to solicit US intervention in Brazil's internal affairs, amid ongoing tensions between the judicial system and the Bolsonaro family. The case reflects broader geopolitical tensions and US sanctions against Brazilian judicial officials.

world-news.title: UK investigating reports Russian warship fired warning shots near yacht in English Channel
world-news.summary: British authorities are investigating an alleged incident in which a Russian naval vessel discharged warning shots near a civilian yacht in the English Channel, raising serious concerns about maritime security and military escalation in European waters. The incident heightens tensions between Russia and NATO members.

world-news.title: Ghosts of empire: A quarantine centre and Laikipia's colonial past
world-news.summary: A US-established Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya's Laikipia region has sparked significant local protests rooted in historical grievances over colonial land appropriation and contemporary sovereignty concerns. The controversy highlights tensions between international health security measures and local autonomy in post-colonial African nations.

world-news.title: UN reports decline in cross-border fire in southern Lebanon
world-news.summary: United Nations observers have documented a reduction in cross-border military exchanges in southern Lebanon, suggesting the recent ceasefire agreement is having measurable effect on ground-level hostilities. The finding provides tentative evidence that the US-Iran truce may be taking hold in this key flashpoint region.

world-news.title: Ebola patients recover in DRC amid claims of outbreak hoax
world-news.summary: Seven patients have been discharged from an Ebola treatment facility in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, though controversies persist regarding the severity of the outbreak and misinformation surrounding the epidemic. The case illustrates challenges in global health communication and public trust in international disease response efforts.

tech-ai.title: SpaceX valuation balloons to $2.6T, briefly passes Amazon
tech-ai.summary: SpaceX's valuation surged to $2.6 trillion following the launch of share trading on Friday, representing a $1 trillion increase and briefly exceeding Amazon's market capitalization. The massive valuation reflects investor confidence in the company's space exploration and satellite internet ambitions.

tech-ai.title: Qualcomm wants to be the chip inside whatever replaces your smartphone, and it just announced two products toward that end
tech-ai.summary: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon announced the company is developing chips for over 40 AI-powered wearable devices, including smart jewelry, camera-equipped earbuds, pins, and watches. The initiative signals Qualcomm's aggressive pivot toward positioning itself as the dominant chipmaker for post-smartphone computing platforms.

tech-ai.title: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
tech-ai.summary: Google has released Android 17 and Wear OS 7 with new floating bubble windows for multitasking, enhanced parental controls, and security improvements alongside expanded Gemini AI integration. The rollout begins with Pixel devices and will expand to other manufacturers throughout 2026.

tech-ai.title: Snap finally debuts its long-awaited AR glasses, Specs, and, oof, they aren't cheap
tech-ai.summary: Snap has officially launched Specs, its consumer augmented reality glasses, priced at $2,195 with preorders opening now and shipping expected in fall 2026. The release marks a significant milestone after over a decade of development and represents Snap's major bet on the AR wearables market.

tech-ai.title: Anthropic's latest feud with the Trump admin may actually help it, sales data suggests
tech-ai.summary: Despite recent tensions with the Trump administration, Anthropic's business adoption continues to grow strongly, according to corporate spending data from Ramp. The controversy may paradoxically be boosting the AI company's appeal and sales momentum with enterprise customers.

tech-ai.title: Bug in FIFA World Cup internal system gave anyone ability to modify TV stream
tech-ai.summary: A security researcher discovered a critical vulnerability in FIFA's online platforms that could have allowed unauthorized access to systems controlling World Cup broadcast streams. The flaw highlights significant security gaps in critical sports infrastructure.

tech-ai.title: The Google / Xreal Aura XR glasses are now available to preorder
tech-ai.summary: The Xreal Aura, the second Android XR device developed in collaboration with Google, is now available for preorder with a $99 refundable deposit. Full retail launch is expected this fall across the United States, United Kingdom, Japan, Canada, and South Korea.

tech-ai.title: Mobileye's US robotaxi launch will put it on both sides of the AV business
tech-ai.summary: Intel's Mobileye is expanding into the robotaxi market with its own US launch, creating potential conflicts of interest with customers it currently supplies autonomous driving technology to. The move signals Mobileye's ambition to vertically integrate into ride-hailing services.

tech-ai.title: Apple plans to change its Hide My Email privacy feature that could make it less effective
tech-ai.summary: Apple will move its Hide My Email feature's anonymously generated email addresses to a different domain in the coming weeks. The change may potentially diminish the effectiveness of the privacy tool.

tech-ai.title: Sixty percent of US consumers say 'AI' in brand messaging is a turnoff, survey finds
tech-ai.summary: A WordPress VIP survey reveals that 60 percent of American consumers view AI mentions in brand marketing negatively, despite growing corporate investment in AI-powered search and content. The finding suggests a significant gap between enterprise AI adoption and consumer sentiment.

crypto-web3.title: Binance may be forced to halt services for EU clients next month: Reuters
crypto-web3.summary: Binance faces potential service disruption in the European Union as it has not yet obtained MiCA authorization ahead of the July 1 regulatory deadline. The world's largest crypto exchange may be forced to cease operations for EU clients if compliance requirements are not met.

crypto-web3.title: Binance says its European regulatory application is compliant despite report of Greek rejection
crypto-web3.summary: Binance asserts its EU crypto license application is compliant despite reports that Greece's financial regulator HCMC intends to reject the application. The dispute highlights ongoing regulatory tensions between the world's largest exchange and European authorities.

crypto-web3.title: Coinbase introduces AI advisor, stock options, and pre-IPO markets in finance push
crypto-web3.summary: Coinbase announced a major expansion into investment and banking services, adding AI-powered advisory tools, derivatives trading, and pre-IPO market access to its platform. The move positions the exchange as a comprehensive financial services provider competing beyond cryptocurrency trading.

crypto-web3.title: Ethereum's biggest protocol overhaul in years moves into its final development stage
crypto-web3.summary: Ethereum's Glamsterdam upgrade, described as the network's largest protocol overhaul in years, has entered its final development phase with closed-environment testing underway. The upgrade represents a significant milestone for the blockchain's technical evolution.

crypto-web3.title: Coinbase to join tokenized stock race with onchain shares, dividend payments
crypto-web3.summary: Coinbase announced it will offer tokenized U.S. stocks on its platform with full dividend payment functionality for holders. The move reflects growing institutional interest in blockchain-based securities and positions Coinbase alongside competitors and traditional finance firms entering the tokenized assets market.

crypto-web3.title: Ripple invests in Flutterwave, pushing its stablecoin and XRP Ledger into payments across Africa
crypto-web3.summary: Ripple has invested in African fintech platform Flutterwave, valuing it at $3.2 billion, and will integrate its RLUSD stablecoin and XRP Ledger for cross-border payments on the continent. The strategic investment positions Ripple to expand blockchain-based payment infrastructure in emerging markets.

crypto-web3.title: U.S. senators urge Treasury not to leave states out of GENIUS Act stablecoin process
crypto-web3.summary: Bipartisan senators led by Republican Cynthia Lummis are pressing the Treasury Department to preserve states' regulatory authority over stablecoins under the proposed GENIUS Act framework. The intervention highlights ongoing federalism debates regarding cryptocurrency oversight in the United States.

crypto-web3.title: State Street targets stablecoin reserve boom with new money market fund
crypto-web3.summary: State Street is launching a money market fund designed to manage reserves backing stablecoins, joining competitors like BlackRock and Franklin Templeton in the growing institutional market. The move reflects increasing adoption of stablecoins and institutional appetite for reserve management services.

crypto-web3.title: Bitcoin miners' AI pivot faces $50 billion reality check, says VanEck
crypto-web3.summary: VanEck reports that cryptocurrency miners pivoting toward AI revenue face significant execution challenges, with investors shifting focus from deal announcements to actual delivery. The analysis indicates the $50 billion opportunity in AI infrastructure may prove difficult for miners to realize.

crypto-web3.title: Hyperliquid, Uniswap and Worldcoin buck crypto slump as traders chase AI, DeFi trends
crypto-web3.summary: Several tokens including Hyperliquid, Uniswap, and Worldcoin outperformed a broader crypto market slump as traders rotated into artificial intelligence and decentralized finance-related assets. The divergence highlights shifting investor preferences within the digital asset space.

cybersecurity.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
cybersecurity.summary: A ransomware and data extortion attack targeting Canvas, a widely-used education technology platform, disrupted classes and coursework at schools and universities across the United States, with attackers threatening to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 institutions. The incident demonstrates the critical vulnerability of essential education infrastructure to coordinated cybercrime operations.

cybersecurity.title: Hackers Used Meta's AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
cybersecurity.summary: High-profile Instagram accounts including those of the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly compromised after attackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant to reset account passwords. The incident highlights how adversaries are adapting attack methods to target automated security systems and underscores vulnerabilities in AI-powered account recovery mechanisms.

cybersecurity.title: New Rokarolla Android Malware Steals PINs, SMS Codes, and Crypto Wallet Funds
cybersecurity.summary: Security researchers have identified Rokarolla, a sophisticated Android banking trojan that targets 217 banking and cryptocurrency applications and includes 137 remote commands, enabling operators to steal lock-screen PINs, intercept SMS codes, and redirect cryptocurrency payments. The malware's extensive command set grants attackers near-complete control over infected devices, posing significant risk to financial and cryptocurrency users.

cybersecurity.title: China-Linked SprySOCKS Backdoor Expands to Windows with Driver-Based Stealth
cybersecurity.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered two previously undocumented Windows variants of the SprySOCKS backdoor, previously thought to be Linux-only, featuring driver-based stealth mechanisms and hard-coded command-and-control configurations. The expansion of this China-linked backdoor to Windows platforms represents an evolution in threat sophistication and persistence capabilities.

cybersecurity.title: Fake Microsoft Alerts Used to Deploy North Korean NarwhalRAT Malware
cybersecurity.summary: North Korean state-sponsored group ScarCruft has been observed conducting spear-phishing campaigns impersonating Microsoft Account security alerts to deliver NarwhalRAT malware to targeted victims. The attack demonstrates how nation-state actors are leveraging trusted brand impersonation to establish initial access and deploy remote access trojans.

cybersecurity.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group 'The Gentlemen?'
cybersecurity.summary: The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting skilled hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy offering affiliates 90 percent of ransom proceeds. An investigation examines available evidence pointing to the real-world identity of the group's administrator.

cybersecurity.title: Attackers Exploit Three Fortinet FortiSandbox Flaws, One Patched Last Week
cybersecurity.summary: Threat intelligence firm Defused Cyber has detected active exploitation of multiple security vulnerabilities in Fortinet FortiSandbox, including CVE-2026-39813 with a critical CVSS score of 9.1, a path traversal flaw in the FortiSandbox JRPC API. The ongoing exploitation of recently patched and unpatched vulnerabilities indicates widespread attack campaigns against this security infrastructure.

cybersecurity.title: Google Vertex AI SDK Flaw Let Attackers Hijack Model Uploads via Bucket Squatting
cybersecurity.summary: A flaw in the Google Cloud Vertex AI SDK for Python allowed attackers without project access to hijack machine learning model uploads and execute code within Google's serving infrastructure through a technique called "Pickle in the Middle." Palo Alto Networks Unit 42, which discovered and reported the vulnerability through Google's bug bounty program, found no evidence of active exploitation.

cybersecurity.title: CISA Flags LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin Flaw Exploited for Root Privilege Escalation
cybersecurity.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency has added CVE-2026-54420, a critical privilege escalation vulnerability in the LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin with a CVSS score of 8.5, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Federal Civilian Executive Branch agencies are required to apply patches by June 18, 2026.

cybersecurity.title: Cisco Releases Security Updates for Actively Exploited SD-WAN Manager Flaw
cybersecurity.summary: Cisco has released security patches for CVE-2026-20262, a medium-severity vulnerability in Catalyst SD-WAN Manager that is actively exploited in the wild, allowing authenticated remote attackers to create files or execute unauthorized operations. The vulnerability carries a CVSS score of 6.5 and affects widely deployed enterprise networking infrastructure.

startups-vc.title: Anthropic’s latest feud with the Trump admin may actually help it, sales data suggests
startups-vc.summary: Anthropic's popularity with business users is growing so well that the latest beef with the government might actually boost it, data from Ramp suggests.

startups-vc.title: Payments startup Flutterwave hits $3.2B valuation, backed by Ripple
startups-vc.summary: African payments infrastructure company Flutterwave has hit a new valuation and landed blockchain company Ripple as an investor and partner.

startups-vc.title: Probably raises $9M to build a more reliable kind of AI
startups-vc.summary: Probably wants to prevent hallucinations and factual errors from reaching users, and achieve accuracy on par with deterministic systems.

startups-vc.title: This startup’s super metals could soon be in military drones, luxury watches, and chef’s knives
startups-vc.summary: Instead of heating metals, Foundation Alloy beats them into submission. The startup has raised $22 million to scale up production of its alloys.

startups-vc.title: Malaysia’s AI agent-powered messaging app Respond.io raises $62.5M, eyes acquisitions
startups-vc.summary: Respond.io, one of Malaysia's startups to watch, uses AI agents to handle high volumes of customer inquiries and charges per convo, not per seat.

startups-vc.title: SpaceX Acquires AI Coding Tool Cursor For $60B In Year’s Largest Startup M&A Deal
startups-vc.summary: The acquisition of Cursor gives SpaceX a foothold into the enterprise software development market, where AI-assisted coding has taken off and led large companies to significantly pare back their reliance on human engineers.

startups-vc.title: Silicon Is Back: Playground Global’s Decade-Long Bet On Hardware, Energy And Deep Tech Looks Prescient
startups-vc.summary: In the decade-plus since Playground Global's founding, it has built its investment thesis around the idea that breakthroughs in science and engineering — not just software — would create the next generation of valuable companies. Company co-founder Peter Barrett breaks it down in this Crunchbase News Q&A.

startups-vc.title: The Crunchbase Tech Layoffs Tracker
startups-vc.summary: Over 127,000 workers at U.S.-based tech companies were laid off in mass job cuts in 2025, per a Crunchbase News tally, and the layoffs have continued into 2026. See the latest companies to cut roles.

startups-vc.title: It Doesn’t Really Matter When Your Competitor Gets Acquired. (Except It Means You Weren’t.)
startups-vc.summary:
There’s a common bit of advice we entrepreneurs and execs always get: worry less about the competition, and more about growing your own business. It’s good advice. But goodness — it’s hard to follow sometimes. Between your competitors’ constant tweets about how everyone loves them, their $50m raise last week, their We Rule the World... 
Continue Reading

startups-vc.title: Dear SaaStr:  My Top Sales Rep Made $600,000 a Year — And Just Quit!  What Happened?
startups-vc.summary:
Dear SaaStr: My Top Sales Rep Made $600,000 a Year — And Just Quit! What Happened? Ok to some of you this will make no sense.  But now I’ve seen this happen again and again, including just the other day at one of my top portfolio companies. The #1 or #2 rep in a start-up,... 
Continue Reading

health-biotech.title: STAT+: How a biotech turned a trial failure into an AI model
health-biotech.summary: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: How a biotech startup turned a failed clinical trial into an AI model, and more.

health-biotech.title: The U.S. infant mortality rate fell to an all-time low, though it still trails other similar nations
health-biotech.summary: Infant mortality in the U.S. dropped to a new all-time low in 2025, according to CDC data, though it still trails other similar nations.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: ARCH-launched Neumora stops depression program
health-biotech.summary: A new AI model to improve trial enrollment, importing drugs from Canada, and more biotech news from The Readout

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about Medicare and obesity drugs, Germany’s pricing plans, and more
health-biotech.summary: Weight loss drugs will be available through Medicare starting next month, thanks to a program that is supposed to be temporary but may be difficult to end

health-biotech.title: How supplement use changed over decades
health-biotech.summary: Medicare-funded weight loss, Utah's lower drunk driving limit, and more health news from Morning Rounds

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Verge Labs’ new AI model solves patient stratification problems for neurology clinical trials
health-biotech.summary: As the saying goes, one man’s trash is another man’s treasure. Or as Verge Labs might put it, one company’s failed clinical trial … is that same company’s new AI…

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Human Cell Atlas leader’s tie to 10x Genomics raises conflict-of-interest questions
health-biotech.summary: As the Human Cell Atlas project marks its 10th anniversary, a leader's ties to a major single-cell RNA sequencing company raise conflict-of-interest questions.

health-biotech.title: Opinion: Congress must reauthorize the Pandemic and All-Hazards Preparedness Act
health-biotech.summary: America’s next health emergency won’t wait for Washington, write W. Craig Vanderwagen and Jennifer B. Alton.

health-biotech.title: Opinion: I’m an Alzheimer’s specialist. I still missed it in my own father
health-biotech.summary: “Alzheimer’s is not primarily a disease of old age. It is a decades-long biological process,” writes Elizabeth Bevins.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: FDA approves Colorado’s plan to import cheaper drugs from Canada
health-biotech.summary: The FDA said it will allow Colorado to import certain prescription drugs from Canada in an effort to bring prices down for residents.

energy-climate.title: Guest post: How a record-high 'energy imbalance' is driving global warming
energy-climate.summary: A analysis examines how record-high energy imbalance in the Earth's atmosphere is accelerating global warming at unprecedented rates. The piece explains the fundamental climate physics driving current warming trends and their implications for climate policy and mitigation efforts.

energy-climate.title: Renewables shield Spain from energy crisis as gas sets electricity price in only 9% of hours
energy-climate.summary: Renewables provided critical protection for Spain during Europe's energy crisis, with gas determining electricity prices in just 9 percent of hours and saving households an average of €10 monthly. The analysis demonstrates the tangible economic and energy security benefits of renewable energy adoption during volatile commodity markets.

energy-climate.title: Power generation's $8.28tn problem – too many plans, not enough buildable work
energy-climate.summary: The renewable energy sector faces a critical infrastructure challenge: converting record investment capital into actual construction and operational capacity. The analysis highlights that capital availability is no longer the bottleneck; execution and project development have become the industry's primary constraint.

energy-climate.title: Vikram Solar to commission 9 GW PV cell manufacturing capacity by December
energy-climate.summary: Indian solar manufacturer Vikram Solar is expanding production capacity significantly, adding 9 GW of cell manufacturing capacity by December 2026 while simultaneously scaling module production from 9.5 GW to 15.5 GW. The expansion reflects strong demand growth in India's renewable energy manufacturing sector.

energy-climate.title: China proposes PV product grading system in bid to drive industry consolidation
energy-climate.summary: China has introduced a three-tier quality classification system for solar modules to standardize performance, reliability, and environmental attributes across the industry. The framework is designed to influence procurement and financing decisions while consolidating China's highly fragmented solar manufacturing sector.

energy-climate.title: Peak Energy, GM partner to scale domestic sodium-ion battery supplies
energy-climate.summary: Peak Energy and General Motors have partnered to accelerate domestic sodium-ion battery manufacturing for AI data centers and grid-scale energy storage applications. The collaboration aims to establish reliable supply chains for next-generation battery technology outside traditional lithium-ion markets.

energy-climate.title: The real cybersecurity debate around chinese inverters is only just beginning
energy-climate.summary: The European Commission's decision to restrict funding for projects using high-risk inverter vendors signals a shift in solar cybersecurity strategy, though experts argue it addresses geopolitical concerns rather than fundamental security challenges. Industry specialists contend that comprehensive solutions require clear technical standards and enhanced asset visibility across the solar sector.

energy-climate.title: Analysis: Energy-efficient air conditioning could save Indian homes 69bn rupees a year
energy-climate.summary: Upgrading to energy-efficient air-conditioning units could save Indian households approximately ₹69 billion ($724 million) annually while reducing electricity demand. The analysis highlights the substantial economic and climate benefits of appliance efficiency improvements in rapidly cooling developing markets.

energy-climate.title: UtmoLight launches new perovskite solar module series
energy-climate.summary: UtmoLight introduced new perovskite solar modules designed to overcome durability and deployment challenges limiting commercial adoption of this emerging technology. The S2 module targets harsh weather conditions with high mechanical load certification, while the lightweight S1 addresses distributed rooftop and building-integrated applications.

energy-climate.title: EU backs Egypt's grid expansion with up to $800m in financing
energy-climate.summary: The European Union has pledged up to €690 million ($800 million) in financing to support Egypt's electricity grid modernization and expansion. The investment reflects international efforts to strengthen grid infrastructure in emerging markets while supporting renewable energy integration across North Africa.

asia-pacific.title: Is the ‘Singapore brand’ losing its shine in China’s food scene?
asia-pacific.summary:
When Celine Teo feels homesick, she visits Singaporean eatery Vanda Room in Beijing’s central business district to enjoy delicacies such as chicken rice and laksa.
“Being able to enjoy a familiar dish abroad always brings a sense of comfort and connection to home,” said the 25-year-old undergraduate, who has lived in China for four years.
But Teo worries these connections are thinning, as some Singapore-linked F&B players retreat, refocus or struggle to defend their edge in China’s increasingly.

asia-pacific.title: Sips of history: how ancient Chinese brewing techniques shaped modern alcohol
asia-pacific.summary:
Alcohol has played a central role in human culture for at least 10,000 years, with the earliest known alcoholic beverage – a rice wine – originating from the Henan province of central China.
However, much of our understanding of these early adult beverages is superficial at best. Recently, a handful of studies from China are shedding light on what the oldest Chinese residents were drinking and how they crafted these libations.
The highlight was the discovery of an unopened bottle of drink from..

asia-pacific.title: What launch of Himars rockets towards Taiwan Strait says about Taipei’s battle plans
asia-pacific.summary:
Taiwan’s recent launch of Himars rockets westwards towards the Taiwan Strait highlights how the island’s defensive strategy is shifting to the use of mobile strike weapons to disrupt a mainland Chinese attack before it reaches shore.
The exercise on June 10 was the first time the High Mobility Artillery Rocket System had been launched in the direction of mainland China. Beijing has yet to respond to the launches.
Analysts said the significance of the drill lay in what it revealed about how...

asia-pacific.title: Can Hong Kong make a giant leap to commercial space insurance?
asia-pacific.summary:
Some may feel a lump in their throats as they watch Hong Kong’s first astronaut, Lai Ka-ying, make history, but few see it as anything more than an inspirational story for our youth. In reality, space, which can feel lofty and far away to pragmatic Hongkongers, could hold the key to our city’s economic future.
In my previous column, we discussed why Hong Kong represents China’s best chance to build an alternative global maritime insurance system, but also why that remains mission impossible for.

asia-pacific.title: Eternal ‘star’: asteroid named after firefighter killed in Hong Kong blaze
asia-pacific.summary:
An asteroid discovered by a Hong Kong-born astronomer has been named after a firefighter who died in the city’s deadliest blaze in decades, with his fiancée expressing gratitude for a move that turned him into an eternal “star”.
Ho Wai‑ho, 37, was killed in the Wang Fuk Court fire in Tai Po last November while helping to evacuate residents.
To honour his sacrifice, amateur astronomer William Yeung Kwong-yu, a former president of the Hong Kong Astronomical Society, has named asteroid 34871...

asia-pacific.title: Gulf gamble: will South Korea step up for Strait of Hormuz security?
asia-pacific.summary:
The expected reopening of the Strait of Hormuz under a US-Iran ceasefire framework could ease pressure on South Korea’s energy-dependent economy while creating a thornier diplomatic problem for Seoul: how much should it contribute to securing the waterway?
That question is likely to shadow President Lee Jae Myung’s visit to the Group of Seven summit this week in Evian-les-Bains, France, where the wars in Ukraine and Iran are expected to dominate talks among G7 leaders and several invited...

asia-pacific.title: American flag blue? Algae turns Trump’s reflecting pool swamp green instead
asia-pacific.summary:
One of President Donald Trump’s pet beauty projects for Washington was supposed to make the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool look blue, like part of the US flag.
Enter Mother Nature. Algae has proliferated in the water, turning it a swampy green.
“Can you see it in my photos? Oh well, I’ll just use a filter to hide the algae,” said Farrah Lu, a 43-year-old tourist from China.
The invasion comes just days after the completion of the pool repainting project, part of Trump’s drive to put his stamp.

asia-pacific.title: SpaceX vaults past Amazon’s market value, briefly topping Microsoft
asia-pacific.summary:
SpaceX roared past Amazon’s market valuation on Tuesday ⁠and briefly topped that of Microsoft, rapidly scaling the list of the world’s most valuable companies on a topsy-turvy trading day fuelled by frenzied action in the firm’s newly listed option contracts.
SpaceX shares rose 4.8 per cent to close at US$201.80, giving Elon Musk’s company a market value of roughly US$2.655 trillion – some US$800 billion more than its value when it sold its record initial public offering last week and about...

asia-pacific.title: Latin America and Europe are bystanders in the US-China AI race, says Lula’s top adviser
asia-pacific.summary:
The race for artificial intelligence has hardened into a two-power contest between the United States and China, fought over rare earths, data and the rules governing the technology, with Latin America and Europe sidelined, Brazil’s top foreign policy adviser said on Tuesday.
Celso Amorim, a former foreign and defence minister, made the case at the Forte de Copacabana International Security Conference in Rio de Janeiro, an annual forum run by the Brazilian Centre for International Relations with.

asia-pacific.title: Europe is gearing up for a trade fight with China. But will it change anything?
asia-pacific.summary:
As trade tensions between Beijing and Brussels continue to rise, China’s firms in the European Union have been forced to walk a delicate tightrope: expanding their presence in the lucrative market while grappling with heightened regulatory hurdles and rapid geopolitical shifts. In the second part of this three-part series, we look at whether China and the EU are heading for a full-blown trade conflict.
Last week, Beijing gathered more than a dozen Chinese companies in Berlin for a forum with a..
